programmes.show_description: Mick has bad news for the Garveys, as he reveals that all of their sunbed shops have burnt down. Joyce struggles for her fourth star, and discovers the true identity of the secret hotel assessor. S5E7
Terry is assigned to be a bodyguard for a Lebanese businessman who is so nervous that he insists on carrying a gun - will it come in handy? S1 E4: The Tethered Goat
When her sister falls ill, Else heads off to Australia. Alf wonders if he married Florence Nightingale. : Outback Bound

Mildred is upset that she has not received an invitation to Norman and Chrissy's wedding, but George has hidden it from her because he wants to go to a darts match. S6 E7: Another Bride, Another Groom
With their affair now exposed, Linda and David plan to run off together - Robert wants to fight David, but Amy decides to foil their plan. S1 E7: Hasta La Vista
In tribute to Ozzy Osbourne. The Osbournes have new neighbours who are playing loud dance music at night; the neighbours refuse to go inside and Ozzy declares war. S1 E4: Won't You Be My Neighbour
Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E2: A Place in the Sun
'70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E3: The Female of the Species
We study the real feel of what it was like to observe the busy railway network of the Midlands in the last decade of steam operations, an era renowned for its heavy industry.
Jet Hawk is cordless, rechargeable and turns any water source into a powerful pressure washer. Call or visit highstreettv.com for more details. Teleshopping.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
When Travis has serious school news that he's scared to break to his mum, Sharon decides she'll pass herself off as Tracey and sort out his problems. S12 E7: Blonde on Blonde
Two sinister men arrive at the Pearces' room - they want their bag back and they aren't above throwing David off the balcony if he doesn't disclose its whereabouts. S3 E5: Costa Del Crime
Winter 1908: with her 21st birthday imminent, Elizabeth appears to be heading for a falling-out with her parents as a consequence of her actions. S1 E12: The Key of the Door
It's the big day for Billy and Julie at the Registrar's office and for Freddie at the Court House. Cousin Eddie arrives from Canada. S4 E2
Harold makes plans to sail around the world with a group of young adventurers, but he has to solve the problem of what to do with Albert. S3 E1: Homes Fit For Heroes
The Minister for Transport is on his way to visit the station, but disaster awaits as there is no milk left at the train's buffet for his cup of tea! S2 E1: No Milk For The Minister
Rigsby turns up at the house with a new sports car apparently purchased in yet another attempt to woo Miss Jones, but his driving doesn't appear to be as stylish as his motor. S3 E3: Clunk Click
It is Chrissy's birthday when she receives a cine camera and projector from her boyfriend Neil, but Larry plans to use it to watch blue movies. S5 E1: The Last Picture Show
Gary's fictitious job with Ron may cover his trips to 1941, but it doesn't pay the bills. The antique trade may provide an answer. S2 E7: Would You Like to Swing on a Star?
Jack is visited by a former schoolmate who is in touch with Matt's nephew Bill - a law student who is in financial trouble - and he loans him the money that he needs. S4 E5: Oh, My Charming Billy Boy
Terry's job as security for the local Rotary Club offers Arthur a chance to gain some much-needed respectability when he is invited to speak at a function. S4 E8: Willesden Suite
John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny and Vince return from an unexpected rendezvous in Paris to pursue their separate careers. S3 E2: Back In London
When George has a nasty cold and gives it to Robin, Chrissy and Jo try to make him better using a range of home-made remedies. S1 E6: Match of the Day
It seems that Mr Rumbold's wife has left him - which has made him more pompous - when he gets a vote of no confidence, and the staff agree to play a darts match with the villagers. S2 E5
When Travis has serious school news that he's scared to break to his mum, Sharon decides she'll pass herself off as Tracey and sort out his problems. S12 E7: Blonde on Blonde
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Harold makes plans to sail around the world with a group of young adventurers, but he has to solve the problem of what to do with Albert. S3 E1: Homes Fit For Heroes
Rigsby attempts to have himself cast as male lead in a play written by effeminate lodger Hilary when he discovers that Ruth is playing the role of the love interest. S3 E2: Stage Struck
Five years have passed when Bob and Terry meet on the last train home by chance and the events of the past few years flood out. S1 E1: Stranger on a Train
A gambler dies owing Arthur Â£2000, but Terry has other problems to contend with when an old flame turns up. With guest star Billy Connolly. S7 E3: Fatal Impression
The Garveys find trouble at Alicante Airport, as Madge's tanning pills are mistaken for illegal drugs, and Mick takes the blame. New family the Dykes, meanwhile, are unimpressed by the Solana. S6 E1
A mishap with baby Katie and a bag of Es results in a stand-off between Mandy and Frank. S5 E6
When Chrissy's mother comes to stay, Jo and Chrissy try to hide the fact that their new flatmate is a man. S1 E2: And Mother Makes Four
When Miss Jones sees a prowler through her window, Rigsby suspects everyone else as a police detective appears and begins to investigate. S1 E6: The Prowler
The Garveys find trouble at Alicante Airport, as Madge's tanning pills are mistaken for illegal drugs, and Mick takes the blame. New family the Dykes, meanwhile, are unimpressed by the Solana. S6 E1
When a young widow is conned out of all of her savings, Arthur sends Terry off to reclaim the money - is there anything he can hold hostage to bait the thief? S1 E5: The Bounty Hunter
With Else off in Australia, Alf has a serious problem. Who will cook his dinner when he gets back from work? S6 E2: Phone Call to Else
The morning after a party, Chrissy and Jo find Robin asleep in their bath and ask him to become their third roommate. S1 E1: Three's a Crowd
With Amy having sent all his clothes to the cleaners, David looks doomed to spend the rest of his holiday in drag and gets no sympathy from his wife or the Cochrans. S2 E1: Manana
In tribute to Ozzy Osbourne. Ozzy is working out with a personal trainer to prepare for an upcoming tour, and Sharon and Kelly go on a shopping spree but lose Ozzy's credit card. S1 E5: Tour of Duty
Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E4: Workers' Playtime
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E5: The Right Spirit
We look at the now-preserved Severn Valley line from Kidderminster to Bewdley and explain why these long-closed railways are still remembered with such affection by those who travelled on them.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Stan applies for a promotion in hopes that he can buy his mum the new coat she needs, but he gets far more than just a raise when he's promoted to assistant inspector. S1 E5: The New Inspector
Stan is put in charge of organising a new cook when the management hand over running the depot canteen to the staff - Blakey is keen on his first appointment, but no-one else is. S1 E6: The Canteen
Rigsby turns up at the house with a new sports car apparently purchased in yet another attempt to woo Miss Jones, but his driving doesn't appear to be as stylish as his motor. S3 E3: Clunk Click
Rigsby is soon calling the Samaritans to stop a suicide attempt when a new lodger arrives at the house, but which of the residents will come off worse? S3 E4: The Good Samaritans
When Miss Jones raises suspicions about new tenant Madeline, Rigsby is not too bothered about her brand of 'modern interpretive dancing' but isn't that keen on her partner. S3 E5: Fawcett's Python
Alan's getting serious about Caroline, but her parents are well-to-do and Rigsby is far from convinced that the young scruff could possibly make the relationship work. S3 E6: The Cocktail Hour
The house is plunged into confusion and mourning when Rigsby discovers basement tenant Mr Osborne stiff as a board. S3 E7: Suddenly At Home
A rude surprise lies in store when Rigsby is a little over-enthusiastic with advice after a couple of newlyweds move into the house. S4 E1: Hello Young Lovers
There's been a spare bed in Philip's room since Alan moved out, and Rigsby has no intentions of keeping it empty. S4 E2: Fire And Brimstone
Rigsby is ecstatic to learn that he has inherited his Uncle George's fortune but distressed at a clause that he must be happily married. S4 E3: Great Expectations
Rigsby decides to move on, give up on Miss Jones and file for divorce - he places an advert in the local newspaper, but his search for love doesn't run entirely smoothly. S4 E4: Pink Carnations
New resident Ambrose claims he has the power of hypnosis due to his gypsy blood - Rigsby is sceptical but soon wonders if Ambrose's abilities could be used in his favour. S4 E5: Under The Influence
Rigsby finally pops the question to Miss Jones with his decree absolute at long last in hand, but will she accept? S4 E6: Come On In The Water's Lovely
The Ropers are invited for a meal not realising that Robin has invited his German friend Franz; the meal does not go well as George has a grudge against Germans. S2 E4: Did You Ever Meet Rommel?
Jo is going on a date with Philip but has forgotten that she is meant to be babysitting; Chrissy agrees to take her place and persuades Robin to come with her. S2 E5: Two Foot Two, Eyes Of Blue
Robin has failed his cookery exam and considers moving back to Southampton, but the girls are far from pleased at the thought of his mate Larry moving in. S2 E6: Carry Me Back to Old Southampton
Larry is staying with Robin having been thrown out of his flat, but Larry moves in when the Ropers decide to convert the attic into another flat. S3 E1: Cuckoo in the Nest
Robin has taken his new girlfriend Angie out on a couple of dates, and now he hopes to bring her back to the flat to have his wicked way with her. S3 E2: Come Into My Parlour
There is a formal dinner dance at the firm where Jo and Chrissy work, but Chrissy's dance partner has injured himself and so Robin becomes her partner instead. S3 E3: I Won't Dance, Don't Ask Me
Jo sees a mouse in her bedroom and refuses to sleep in there; much to Chrissy's dismay, Jo swaps with Robin while the mouse is on the loose. S3 E4: Of Mice And Women
Chrissy receives flowers and chocolates from a secret admirer; it turns out that they were meant for Jo and that the admirer had wrongly assumed her to be Chrissy. S3 E5: Somebody Out there Likes Me
As the much-loved sitcom celebrates ten years on TV screens, Angus Deayton presents a trip down memory lane with the help of interviews with the cast and crew, iconic clips, and unseen footage.
A video implicating two senior police officers in the Serious Crime division is mistakenly returned to Daley Videos, and Arthur and Terry get involved. S7 E4: The Last Video Show
A new barman asks for work at the Solana, putting Mateo into a panic, and sending Leslie on a mission to help him. Michael continues to be led astray by Tiger, as Mick finally leaves jail. S6 E2
A gas leak at the Gallaghers' brings sexy fireman Sean to Ian and Mandy's attention. But which of them will win the race to get fireman Sean into bed? S5 E7
Robin cooks a pork meal for Jo and her date not realising that her dinner guest is Jewish. S1 E3: Some Enchanted Evening
An election polarises the household - Rigsby supports the Conservatives, Alan and Philip lean towards Labour, and Miss Jones is impressed with the Liberal candidate. S1 E7: Stand Up and Be Counted
A new barman asks for work at the Solana, putting Mateo into a panic, and sending Leslie on a mission to help him. Michael continues to be led astray by Tiger, as Mick finally leaves jail. S6 E2
